title: Syzygium khaoyaiense
taxonomy: Syzygium khaoyaiense belongs to the family Myrtaceae, a large group of flowering plants that includes eucalyptus, guava, and clove. This species is part of the genus Syzygium, which includes about 1200 species of trees and shrubs native to tropical and subtropical regions. Syzygium khaoyaiense is a species of evergreen tree that is endemic to Thailand. The classification of Syzygium khaoyaiense reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Myrtaceae family. Further research is needed to fully understand the classification and relationships of this species.

intro: Syzygium khaoyaiense, also known as the Khao Yai Syzygium, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae, native to Thailand. The plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to protect against cell damage, reduce inflammation, and prevent the growth of pathogens. The bioactive constituents of Syzygium khaoyaiense include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as tea, decoctions, and infusions, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
